settings.title: No Added Sugar
settings.text: Zero added sugar, zero artificial sweeteners. Just clean hydration you can feel good about.

settings.title: Non-GMO & Gluten Free
settings.text: Clean ingredients you can trust. SolarSea Sport certified Non-GMO and gluten free — backed by independent testing.

settings.title: Natural Electrolytes
settings.text: A naturally occurring spectrum of sodium, potassium, magnesium, chloride, and trace minerals — absorbed the way your body was designed.

settings.title: GRAS Certified Minerals
settings.text: Independently lab tested. Heavy metals screened. Classified as Generally Recognized As Safe for food and beverage products.

settings.heading: Real Minerals. Real Hydration.
settings.description: Most electrolyte drinks are built in labs using synthetic powders. Hydra is different. Our electrolytes come from natural sea mineral deposits sourced from the Great Salt Lake — one of the most mineral-rich inland bodies of water in the world.

settings.question: How do I use Hydra?
settings.answer: Hydra Pops: Freeze and enjoy as a hydrating popsicle, or drink straight from the pouch as a liquid shot. Hydra Drops: Add a few drops to any beverage — water, sparkling water, juice, smoothies — for an instant electrolyte boost.

settings.question: What does it taste like?
settings.answer: Hydra Pops come in three refreshing flavors — Apple, Strawberry, and Lime — made with real fruit juice. Hydra Drops are flavorless and odorless, so they blend seamlessly into any drink without changing the taste.

settings.question: Is it safe?
settings.answer: Absolutely. Our sea mineral electrolyte source (SolarSea Sport) is classified as GRAS — Generally Recognized As Safe — by the FDA. It's independently lab tested for heavy metals, microplastics, and contaminants.

settings.question: Is this the same as sea salt?
settings.answer: No. While both come from natural sources, Hydra uses a concentrated mineral extract from the Great Salt Lake that delivers a full spectrum of electrolytes — sodium, potassium, magnesium, chloride, and 72+ trace minerals. It's not just salt — it's a complete mineral complex.

settings.question: What about microplastics?
settings.answer: The Great Salt Lake is an inland body of water, naturally isolated from ocean plastic contamination. Our mineral source is also independently tested to confirm it's free from microplastic content.

settings.question: What about toxins in the Great Salt Lake?
settings.answer: Every batch of our sea mineral concentrate is independently tested for heavy metals, environmental contaminants, and microbial purity. The mineral extraction process concentrates beneficial electrolytes while screening out harmful substances.
